data.narrative:
Fire Point, a Series A defense and security company founded by Denys Shtilerman, Iryna Terekh, and Yehor Skalyha, is evaluated against a universe of 10 private and 2 public comparables in the Defense & Security sector. The company has secured undisclosed funding with the Ukrainian Ministry of Defence as an anchor investor.

Our valuation relies on peer-median analysis across three same-stage, same-sector private companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). This peer set yields a median valuation of $300M, with the broader comparable universe spanning $150M to $500M. The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given available data constraints and Fire Point's stage positioning within an established cohort of early-stage defense tech investors.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This median reflects Fire Point's positioning within the Defense & Security Series A market. Key uncertainties include the degree to which the Ukrainian MoD anchor relationship commands a valuation premium or discount relative to comparable-stage peers, the company's specific revenue traction and burn metrics relative to the three direct comps, and the relevance of the broader private and public comparable set (which includes later-stage firms like Anduril and Scale AI) to establishing true Series A market pricing. Limited transparency on round terms and company financials constrains confidence in the range.
